shortAnswer: Non-HDL cholesterol includes all cholesterol types except the "good" HDL, so mainly LDL and other vessel-damaging particles. It's considered a good overall indicator of cardiovascular risk, especially when triglycerides are elevated.
metaDescription: Non-HDL cholesterol elevated? Meaning for cardiovascular risk explained simply.
doctorQuestions: What is my cardiovascular risk based on this value?
Should my cholesterol therapy be adjusted?
